title: ochna boiviniana
intro: Ochna boiviniana, also known as the "Mangapot" or " Poison Berry", is a tropical plant species native to the island of Madagascar. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Ochna boiviniana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling its leaves or roots, or as a tincture by soaking the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
